title: Assign Item Urgency Level
description: The system shall allow a user to assign an urgency level (1-5) to an item, where 1 is the lowest urgency and 5 is the highest.
rationale: This requirement enables users to prioritize items based on their urgency, enhancing workflow efficiency by allowing quick identification of critical tasks.
business_value: Improves task management and productivity by ensuring that high-priority tasks are addressed promptly, leading to better resource utilization and faster project completion.

source.description: As a user I need to assign an urgency level (1-5) so that I can prioritize items based on urgency.

acceptance_criteria: Given an item exists with no urgency level assigned
When I assign urgency level 3 to that item
Then the item's urgency level is set to 3
postconditions: The item's urgency level is updated to the specified value
functional_constraints: Urgency levels must be numeric values between 1 and 5 inclusive
Items can have their urgency level set or changed as needed
edge_cases: Assigning an urgency level outside the range of 1 to 5
Attempting to assign a non-numeric value as an urgency level

interfaces.name: Item Management API
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the Item Management API to update the urgency level of items.

test_cases.description: Assigning a valid urgency level (3) to an item
test_cases.expected_result: The item's urgency level is successfully set to 3

test_cases.description: Attempting to assign an invalid urgency level (6)
test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating that the urgency level must be between 1 and 5

test_cases.description: Attempting to assign a non-numeric value as an urgency level ('three')
test_cases.expected_result: Error message indicating that the urgency level must be a numeric value
